outbound_official.note: No single consolidated outbound visa-free list page for Czech ordinary-passport citizens located on any Czech government domain. The Czech MFA (mzv.gov.cz) publishes per-country visa/entry information for Czech travellers in the 'státy světa — informace na cesty' country encyclopedia (https://mzv.gov.cz/jnp/cz/encyklopedie_statu/index.html); each country entry includes a 'Víza — režim vstupu' (Visa — entry regime) subsection. There is no India-style single-page outbound aggregation. The 'cestujeme' travel portal (https://mzv.gov.cz/cestujeme) also provides country-by-country lookup but no consolidated list. Rely on destination-side data and the per-country MFA encyclopedia entries.

Citizens of EU, EEA and Schengen member states enjoy freedom of movement and are not subject to visa requirements. This covers all EU member states plus Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland.

visa_policy.notes: Czechia is a Schengen member. Visa-free regime applies only to short-term non-profit stays up to 90 days within any 180-day period. For employment, a Schengen employment visa is required even for visa-free nationalities. The Czech official page lists 195 countries with a visa obligation (list last updated February 9, 2026). No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists; visas are physical stickers issued at embassies/consulates. Eight countries have bilateral Czech-specific visa waiver agreements giving additional stay beyond Schengen 90/180 rule: Argentina, Chile, Israel, South Korea, Costa Rica, Malaysia, Uruguay (each +90 additional days, Czech Republic only) and Singapore (+30 days, up to +60 days in Czech Republic only). Entry/Exit System (EES) launched progressively from October 12, 2025 at Schengen external borders. ETIAS travel authorisation for visa-exempt nationals expected later in 2026 — check official sources for activation date. Visa regime for diplomatic/service passport holders may differ.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by an EU Member State (excluding Ireland and Denmark)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit in another EU Member State, or EU citizens' family members holding a residence card or permanent residence card in another EU Member State, may stay in the Czech Republic without a visa for up to 90 days within any 180-day period (non-profit purposes). Residence permits issued by Ireland and Denmark are excluded. Family members of EU citizens whose short-term visa has expired may also stay up to 3 months if they qualify as spouse, parent, or dependent offspring of an EU citizen.

credential.subtype: long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of scientific research issued by another EU Member State

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of scientific research issued by another EU Member State (excluding Ireland and Denmark) may stay in the Czech Republic without a visa for up to 1 year from first entry, provided they are conducting scientific research in Czechia for the same purpose. Must register with Foreign Police within 3 days. Permit/visa must remain valid throughout the stay. Research organisation must notify the Ministry of the Interior. Family members holding a long-term residence permit from another EU Member State may also accompany without a visa.

credential.subtype: long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of studies (exchange programme) issued by another EU Member State

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of studies issued by another EU Member State (excluding Ireland and Denmark), where the permit was issued under an exchange programme including mobility programmes (e.g. Erasmus) or under agreements between universities (the permit must contain a note about the exchange programme), may stay in the Czech Republic without a visa for up to 1 year from first entry, provided they are studying at a university in Czechia. Must register with Foreign Police within 3 days. Permit/visa must remain valid throughout the stay. University must notify the Ministry of the Interior.

Holders of diplomatic, service, official or special passports from countries that have concluded bilateral visa waiver agreements with the Czech Republic are exempt from the visa requirement for short stays. The specific countries and maximum stay periods are governed by individual bilateral treaties. The visa regime for holders of diplomatic, service and special passports may differ from ordinary passport holders — holders are advised to contact the respective Czech consulate to confirm. The 'Countries with Visa Waiver Regime' page (mzv.gov.cz) lists these bilateral agreements but the full per-country list could not be rendered due to JavaScript gating.

cbi.notes: Czech Republic does not offer a Citizenship by Investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is available through naturalization after qualifying residency periods, or by descent. No official government source confirms any direct investment-for-citizenship pathway. Confirmed via ipc.gov.cz and mzv.gov.cz.

programs.name: Long-Term Residence Permit for the Purpose of Investing
programs.notes: Minimum investment of CZK 75 million (confirmed current as of 2026). Must create at least 20 new jobs for EU citizens or their family members for a minimum of 2 years. Up to 60% of financial investment may be satisfied through tangible or intangible assets (e.g., technologies, know-how). Permit valid for up to 2 years, renewable for up to 2 years at a time. Processing: 30 days standard, 60 days for complex cases. Administrative fee: 2,500 CZK (in-country) or 5,000 CZK (from abroad). Available to businesspeople (natural persons) or partners/members of statutory bodies/authorised representatives of commercial corporations. Also accessible via czechinvest.gov.cz.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Program
programs.eligibility: Highly qualified IT and marketing professionals. Two categories: (1) employees of a foreign company working remotely in Czechia who remain employed abroad; (2) freelancers holding Czech business license in IT or marketing. Available to nationals of: Australia, Brazil, India, Israel, Japan, Canada, South Korea, Mexico, New Zealand, Singapore, UK, USA, and Taiwan. Applicants must not have held a valid Czech long-term visa or residence permit within the preceding year.
programs.notes: Established by Czech Government Decree No. 475 of June 28, 2023. Confirmed active as of July 2025 per MPO website. Administered by Ministry of Industry and Trade (digitalnomad@mpo.gov.cz). Family members (spouse, registered partner, children) may apply simultaneously. Inclusion valid for maximum 1 year. No legal entitlement to participation. Long-term visa or residence permit issued after approval.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Worker Programme
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals in positions classified as CZ-ISCO main classes 1-3 (managerial, professional, technical). Employer must have operated in Czech Republic for at least 2 years, settled financial obligations, and employ minimum 3 people. Salary must meet average income for relevant CZ-ISCO sub-group or Blue Card minimum. Sectors: manufacturing, services, public sector (excluding medical).
programs.notes: Administered by Ministry of Industry and Trade (vysocekvalifikovani@mpo.gov.cz) or Ministry of Health for healthcare (programzdravotnictvi@mzcr.cz). Inclusion valid for 1 year. Enables priority employee card application at Czech embassies/consulates abroad. Family members included. No legal entitlement to participation. Contact for investors: afc-visa@czechinvest.gov.cz.

programs.name: Key and Research Staff Programme
programs.eligibility: Statutory representatives, managers and key specialists of significant Czech and foreign investors, research organisations, technological companies and start-ups. Employees in intra-corporate transfer (managerial/specialist) or newly hired in CZ-ISCO classes 1-3. Workers from all third countries.
programs.notes: Administered by Ministry of Industry and Trade. Programme modified June 1, 2026 — expanded provisions for Ukrainian citizens with specific residency and documentation requirements. Contact: klicovypersonal@mpo.gov.cz (investors), programklicovy@czechinvest.org (research/tech/start-ups). Inclusion valid for 1 year; subsequent additions require only application form and salary declaration. Benefits include 30-day visa processing, priority submission at Czech embassies abroad, family member inclusion, and simplified documentation.

programs.name: EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with completed higher vocational or university education of at least 3 years. Must work in a position requiring high qualifications (CZ-ISCO 1-3) with a work contract of at least 1 year. Salary must be at least 1.5 times the average gross salary in the Czech Republic.
programs.notes: Long-term residence permit enabling third-country citizens to stay and work in Czechia for more than 3 months in a highly qualified position. Administered by Ministry of the Interior. Also documented at ipc.gov.cz and mv.gov.cz.

validation_notes: Updated notes for 8 countries with bilateral Czech-specific visa waiver agreements to reflect additional stay beyond Schengen 90/180 rule: Argentina, Chile, Israel, South Korea, Costa Rica, Malaysia, Uruguay (+90 additional days each in CZ only) and Singapore (+30 days, up to +60 days in CZ only). Previous record did not capture this distinction.
